main_sections.title: The Swing
main_sections.poem_text:
How do you like to go up in a swing,
Up in the air so blue?
Oh, I do think it the pleasantest thing,
Ever a child can do!

Up in the air and over the wall,
Till I can see so wide,
Rivers and trees and cattle and all,
Over the countryside.

Till I look down on the garden green,
Down on the roof so brown,
Up in the air I go flying again,
Up in the air and down!

– Robert Louis Stevenson
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: The poem celebrates the joy of swinging — the simple, exhilarating pleasure of going up in a swing. The tone is light, carefree, and full of childlike delight. The central imagery is of ascending into the open blue sky, rising above walls, and glimpsing a wide rural landscape — rivers, trees, cattle, fields, a green garden, a brown roof — before swooping back down. The dominant device is repetition: the phrase 'Up in the air' recurs to mirror the rhythmic back-and-forth of the swing itself. The poem's rhyme scheme (ABAB) reinforces this playful rhythm. The theme is the sheer joy of childhood freedom and the wonder of seeing the world from a high vantage point.

oracy.section_name: Let us Recite + Let us Think B + Let us Listen

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Recite: Read aloud and recite the poem 'The Swing' by Robert Louis Stevenson (p.103).

Let us Think B. Think and discuss.
1. Have you seen a swing? Where have you seen it? What was it made of?
2. What would you like to see when you are up in the air on a swing?

question_bank.stem: Have you seen a swing? Where have you seen it? What was it made of?
What would you like to see when you are up in the air on a swing?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Listen: Your teacher will read out the poem again. Listen carefully and make a list of words that rhyme. You may add two more words of your choice as well.
Rhyming words in 'The Swing': swing–thing, blue–do, wall–all, wide–countryside, green–seen, brown–down. Add two more rhyming words of your choice.

question_bank.stem: Listen to the poem read aloud. List the rhyming word pairs you hear. Then add two more rhyming words of your own choice.

tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: How do you like to go up in a swing, / Up in the air so blue? / Oh, I do think it the pleasantest thing, / Ever a child can do! // Up in the air and over the wall, / Till I can see so wide, / Rivers and trees and cattle and all, / Over the countryside. // Till I look down on the garden green, / Down on the roof so brown, / Up in the air I go flying again, / Up in the air and down! — Robert Louis Stevenson

reading.section_name: New Words + Let us Think A

tasks_verbatim.task_text: New Words: pleasantest, cattle, countryside. Read and understand these words from the poem.

Let us Think A. Answer the following.
1. What does the poet see when the swing goes over the wall?
2. Does the poet enjoy swings? Which lines tell us that?
3. 'Up in the air so blue' — Why do you think the poet says the air is blue?

question_bank.stem: What does the poet see when the swing goes over the wall?
Does the poet enjoy swings? Which lines tell us that?
'Up in the air so blue' — Why do you think the poet says the air is blue?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Write 1: The word 'pleasantest' means enjoyable. There are many things that people enjoy doing. Write a short paragraph about the activities you enjoy doing in your free time.

question_bank.stem: Write a short paragraph about the activities you enjoy doing in your free time.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Write 2: Look at the picture carefully. Fill in the blanks with suitable prepositions from the box: on, beside, between, above, in front of, behind, under.
1. There is a ladybug ........................... the two flower pots.
2. A butterfly is flying ........................... the flower.
3. A honeybee is sitting .......................... the flower.
4. The watercan is ........................... the flower pots.
5. An ant is walking ........................... the leaf.
6. A caterpillar is walking ........................... a flowerpot.
7. A leaf has fallen ........................... the flower pot.

question_bank.stem: Fill in: There is a ladybug ......... the two flower pots. (on / beside / between / above / in front of / behind / under)
Fill in: A butterfly is flying ......... the flower.
Fill in: A honeybee is sitting ......... the flower.
Fill in: The watercan is ......... the flower pots.
Fill in: An ant is walking ......... the leaf.
Fill in: A caterpillar is walking ......... a flowerpot.
Fill in: A leaf has fallen ......... the flower pot.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Illustration of a garden scene with flower pots, a ladybug, butterfly, honeybee, watercan, ant, caterpillar, and a fallen leaf — used to answer preposition fill-in questions

word_work.section_name: Let us Learn + Let us Listen + Let us Speak

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn — Stringing together: Expand each sentence step by step, making each new sentence longer than the previous one. Example: Jeevan waited. → Jeevan waited for the bus. → Jeevan waited for the bus all morning. → Jeevan waited for the bus all morning in the rain. → Jeevan waited for the bus all morning in the rain because his scooter was in the garage being repaired.
Now frame a set of sentences in the same way. Make sure that each sentence is longer than the previous one.

question_bank.stem: Frame a set of expanding sentences as shown: each sentence must be longer than the previous one (sentence expansion / stringing together).

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Listen — Verbs and Nouns: Read the following sentence: 'I swing on a swing.' The first 'swing' is a verb (to move forward and backward); the second 'swing' is a noun (an object). Many words can be used both as a verb and a noun. Read the following sentences. Circle the verbs and underline the nouns. One has been done for you.
A. dance: 'The dance begins in an hour.' / 'My friends and I dance when we are happy.'
B. play: 'I like to play kho-kho.' / 'We are going to watch a play tomorrow.'
C. answer: 'Can you answer the questions?' / 'I know the answer.'
D. fly: 'There is a fly on the wall.' / 'My friends fly kites on 15th August.'
E. waves: 'The waves did not let us finish our sandcastle.' / 'She waves to her siblings while leaving for school.'

question_bank.stem: Read the sentence pair for 'dance'. Circle the verb and underline the noun: 'The dance begins in an hour.' / 'My friends and I dance when we are happy.'
Read the sentence pair for 'play'. Circle the verb and underline the noun: 'I like to play kho-kho.' / 'We are going to watch a play tomorrow.'
Read the sentence pair for 'answer'. Circle the verb and underline the noun: 'Can you answer the questions?' / 'I know the answer.'
Read the sentence pair for 'fly'. Circle the verb and underline the noun: 'There is a fly on the wall.' / 'My friends fly kites on 15th August.'
Read the sentence pair for 'waves'. Circle the verb and underline the noun: 'The waves did not let us finish our sandcastle.' / 'She waves to her siblings while leaving for school.'

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Speak — Tongue twister and /W/ phonics drill:
a) Read aloud the tongue twister: 'How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood?' Make an 'O' shape with your lips while pronouncing the 'W' sound. Repeat 4–5 times.
b) Read aloud the following words making an 'O' shape for 'W': fever, fewer, valley, white, vine, wise, win, wait, vet, wet.

question_bank.stem: Read aloud the tongue twister 'How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood?' making an 'O' shape with your lips for the /W/ sound. Repeat 4–5 times.
Read aloud these words making an 'O' shape for /W/: fever, fewer, valley, white, vine, wise, win, wait, vet, wet.

beyond_text.section_name: Let us Do + Let us Explore

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Do — Swings of Imagination: Draw a magical swing that can take you anywhere (outer space, a jungle, a book world). Then write a short paragraph about the adventure.

question_bank.stem: Draw a magical swing that can take you anywhere. Then write a short paragraph about the adventure you would have.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Explore: There are some festivals or months in which people like swinging on swings. Collect some information about those festivals or months, and share it with the class.

question_bank.stem: Find out which festivals or months are associated with swinging on swings in India. Collect information and share with the class.
